data.narrative:
Loft Orbital is a Series C-stage Space & Aerospace company with a current post-money valuation of $1.0B and $326M in total raised capital. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace) and 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two quantitative methods were applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $1.0B last post-money valuation and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $1.7B. Peer-median triangulation across the four same-stage private companies yields $1.3B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring using the Space & Aerospace sector P/S median of 42.9x could not be applied, as the target's revenue is not disclosed. Revenue disclosure would be required to complete this method.

The two applicable approaches triangulate to a range of $1.1B to $2.0B, with a median of $1.5B. This range reflects the $1.3B peer median and $1.7B stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among peer valuations—particularly Sierra Space's $8.0B outlier—and the absence of revenue metrics needed to ground the public-comp anchor, which carries a 42.9x median multiple in the sector.
